---
title: "ell_add_self_loops"
generated: true
description: "Append a self-loop `(i, i)` to every row of an ELL adjacency."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.sparse.ell`](../nitrix-sparse-ell.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:ell_add_self_loops -->
```python
ell_add_self_loops(ell: ELL, edge_attr: Optional[Float[Array, 'm k_max f']] = None, *, fill: Literal['mean', 'zero', 'add'] = 'mean', self_value: float = 1.0) -> Tuple[ELL, Optional[Float[Array, 'm k_max_plus_1 f']]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:ell_add_self_loops -->
Append a self-loop `(i, i)` to every row of an ELL adjacency.

Returns a new ELL with one extra neighbour slot per row whose index
is the row itself (`indices[i, -1] = i`) and whose `values` entry
is `self_value` -- a non-identity marker so the slot reads as a real
edge (e.g. it survives the padding mask of
[`ell_row_softmax`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)), not as a pad.  `k_max`
grows by one.

Self-loops are a graph-convolution construct, not part of the geometric
mesh adjacency: [`mesh_k_ring_adjacency`](../nitrix-sparse.qmd) and friends
are self-loop-free.
Graph attention attends each vertex to its own features -- the
neighbourhood in Velickovic et al. (2018) explicitly *includes* node
`i` -- and the GCN renormalisation trick (Kipf & Welling 2017) adds
the self-connection `A_hat = A + I`.  A convolution that wants either
adds the self-edge to the bare adjacency before aggregating; without it
the row's reduction (and any row-softmax over it) simply omits the
self-term, which is a different operator.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:ell_add_self_loops -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ell` | `ELL` | — | — | Adjacency in ELL format, assumed self-loop-free (the geometric mesh case). If a row already contains an `(i, i)` edge it is not removed; a duplicate self-slot is appended. |
| `edge_attr` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'm k_max f']]` | — | `None` | Optional per-edge attributes, shape `(m, k_max, *f)` aligned with the ELL pattern. `None` skips attribute handling. |
| `fill` | `Literal['mean', 'zero', 'add']` | — | `'mean'` | Self-edge attribute rule; see above. Ignored when `edge_attr` is `None`. |
| `self_value` | `float` | — | `1.0` | Scalar written into the self-slot of `values`. Must differ from `ell.identity` for the slot to read as a real edge (the default `1.0` is correct for every built-in algebra except a degenerate `identity == 1`). |

**Returns** `Tuple[ELL, Optional[Float[Array, 'm k_max_plus_1 f']]]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:ell_add_self_loops -->
### Per edge attributes

When `edge_attr` is supplied (the per-edge vector tensor consumed by
[`semiring_ell_edge_aggregate`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)), the synthesised
self-edge needs an
attribute.  Lacking an intrinsic self-feature, `fill` derives one
from the row's existing **valid** (non-pad) edges:

- `'mean'` -- the per-row mean of the vertex's other edge attributes;
  the natural default when no self-feature is defined.
- `'add'`  -- the per-row sum.
- `'zero'` -- a zero vector.

Padding slots are excluded from the reduction via `ell.values !=
ell.identity` (so a partially-padded row averages only its real
edges).  `fill` applies only to `edge_attr`; the scalar `values`
self-slot is always `self_value` (the geometric-weight channel,
typically overwritten downstream -- e.g. by attention weights).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
